articles.title: Kaity Cimo is running the Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer’s Association with her mother in mind
articles.description:
Cimo is running for the Alzheimer's Association for her mom who was diagnosed with the disease.

The post Kaity Cimo is running the Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer’s Association with her mother in mind appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Kaity Cimo is running the Boston Marathon to support the Alzheimer's Association.
Cimo's mother was diagnosed with Alzheimer's, motivating her participation.
Funds raised will aid Alzheimer's research and support local families affected.

articles.title: On eve of trade deadline, Bruins make a horrid case for becoming buyers
articles.description:
With Thursday's loss to a rebuilding Nashville team, Boston is just 11-14-4 on the road this season.

The post On eve of trade deadline, Bruins make a horrid case for becoming buyers appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Bruins lose to Nashville, worsening their road record to 11-14-4 this season.
Trade deadline approaches, raising concerns about team performance and potential acquisitions.
Local fans worry about the team's competitiveness and playoff chances amid ongoing struggles.

articles.title: Judge pauses deportation of Boston area Irish man held in custody for 6 months
articles.description:
A U.S. Court of Appeals judge granted Seanys Culleton’s stay of removal pending the resolution of his petition for review. 

The post Judge pauses deportation of Boston area Irish man held in custody for 6 months appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: U.S. Court of Appeals pauses deportation of Seanys Culleton for review.
Culleton has been in custody for 6 months awaiting resolution.
This decision affects Boston's Irish community and immigration policy discussions.

articles.title: Victim in early morning Foxborough fire identified
articles.description:
The fire broke out early Tuesday morning and killed a man in his 80s and his dog, according to fire officials.

The post Victim in early morning Foxborough fire identified appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Fire in Foxborough early Tuesday morning killed a man in his 80s and his dog.
Victim identified as a local resident, highlighting fire safety concerns in the area.
Incident raises awareness about fire prevention measures for Boston-area residents.

articles.title: Boston tree canopy has expanded, in step with plans for a cleaner, greener city
articles.description:
A new report unveiled Thursday shows that the city’s tree coverage expanded by more than 150 acres between 2019 and 2024.

The post Boston tree canopy has expanded, in step with plans for a cleaner, greener city appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston's tree coverage increased by over 150 acres from 2019 to 2024.
This expansion supports the city's initiative for a cleaner, greener environment.
Residents can expect improved air quality and enhanced urban aesthetics.

articles.title: Kraft agrees to help cover World Cup security costs, but Foxbourough leaders still unsatisfied
articles.description:
"Any suggestion that BS26 or the Kraft Group have adequately addressed the Town's concerns is false," Foxborough Select Board Chair Bill Yukna said.

The post Kraft agrees to help cover World Cup security costs, but Foxbourough leaders still unsatisfied appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Kraft Group will assist with World Cup security costs for Foxborough.
Foxborough Select Board Chair Bill Yukna claims concerns remain unaddressed.
Local leaders express dissatisfaction, impacting community trust in event management.

articles.title: Two Saints Tavern, a familiar bar for Northeastern students, is closing after 10 years in Fenway
articles.description:
“The system is broken, but it’s not their fault,” the bar's managing partner said.

The post Two Saints Tavern, a familiar bar for Northeastern students, is closing after 10 years in Fenway appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Two Saints Tavern will close after 10 years serving Northeastern students in Fenway.
The bar's managing partner cited systemic issues as a reason for the closure.
Local students will lose a popular gathering spot in the Fenway area.
